Zinc..personality: A deeply enfeebled, nervous constitution marked by extreme sensitivity to all sensory stimuli—noise, light, conversation—combined with an underlying slowness and sluggishness of all mental and physical functions. The patient appears prematurely aged, pallid, and withered, with a docile temperament that becomes irritable when provoked.
Zinc..mental_state: The mind is slow, weak, and forgetful, with poor memory and a characteristic need to have questions repeated before comprehending and answering them—staring blankly before the face lights up with understanding. There is marked mental prostration and exhaustion, progressing from dullness and stupor toward unconsciousness in severe states, yet with paradoxical startling and hypersensitivity to minor stimuli.
Zinc..emotional_pattern: The core emotional pattern is one of nervous exhaustion manifesting as oversensitivity and irritability—morose when others talk, distressed by conversation, yet fundamentally docile and compliant until pushed. There is a restless, fidgety quality especially in the extremities (notably the feet), reflecting an inner agitation trapped within a depleted system that cannot adequately discharge its tension.
